title: France: Provence, Verdon, Drôme, Vercors & Burgundy

main_route.inference_reason: default England-France crossing; departure and crossing fall in the previous diary window, no port named in these pages

main_route.excerpt: We just got it dry enough to hang in the van before walking off to supper in Esparron.
We stopped in Riez to buy pastries, olive oil + wine.
At 6.15. we set off for Moustiers, about 15min walk along the road.
We are in __Chaplains__ * * * (? *) campsite at Sailline.
This is a very pretty campsite outside __St. Agnan__ Village, v. small, no [?accueil].
We drove N.E. ~~down~~ up, Gorge de la Bourne, which had a steep, overhanging roof on the other side
At 5.3[?0] we got to St. Jean de [?lux] (near [~~Chalon~~] Chalon) & went for a swim in the fabulously clean pool
We said goodbye to the cat-lovers next door... + drove to [strikethrough:St] __Beaune__. We wandered around the __Hotel-Dieu__.
We reached [?Guignicourt] + got the perfect spot by the river.

main_route.original_wording: L'Ascenseur à Bateaux des Fontinettes (Arques near St. Omer)
main_route.excerpt: As we reached SE of __St. Omer__... went to look at L'Ascenseur à Bateaux des Fontinettes, a pool of water Takes boats up a canal, built in 1887. on the Canal Neufosse.

crossings.excerpt: No outbound crossing narrated in this window; this is a continuation of an ongoing France trip whose departure falls in the previous diary window. Default England-France crossing assumed.

excursions.label: Day trip into the Gorges du Verdon (Routes des Crêtes loop)

stops.normalized_name: Couloir Samson, Gorges du Verdon, France
stops.excerpt: parked at Couloir Sampson (columns of the temple) & walked to see Baume (cave) of Pigeons through dark tunnels
we went over Pont de l'Artuby, the highest bungee jump in Europe (fortunately not working)
Then we canoed down the Drome in 2 single canoes + a double. It was fabulous, canoeing - lots of rapids

excursions.label: Vercors Resistance day: La Chapelle, Luire Cave & Vassieux museum

stops.excerpt: __Drove into La Chapelle__ to buy & eat breakfast. A lovely saxophone solo at the start of the service.
We drove off to __Luire Cave__. It was considered one of the deepest in the world – very cold, always 7°.
Musée de la Résistance, Vassieux-en-Vercors, Drôme / Vercors

excursions.label: Côte Chalonnaise wine & swimming days (Givry, Saint-Jean-de-Vaux pool)

stops.excerpt: PERCEPTION DE GIVRY / PISCINE DE ST-JEAN-DE-VAUX / Carte d'Abonnement
Chalon was the place that Niepce came from... We visited + tasted wine at Isaie.

boundary_flags.issue: Window opens mid-trip: the family is already camped at Esparron and the diary continues an ongoing France holiday. The home departure and Channel crossing are in the previous diary window, so seq 1 (home) and seq 2 (Dover/crossing) are reconstructed, not narrated here.

boundary_mixed=true: the window ends at the Fontinettes boat-lift / St. Omer visit on Fri 12 Aug; the family has not yet returned home. The northbound run to the Channel and home falls in the next diary window.

summary: A 2005 summer leg of a long France camping holiday, picking up in early August with the family already based in Provence near Esparron-de-Verdon. They move to Moustiers-Sainte-Marie for the Gorges du Verdon, then north into the Drôme valley for canoeing at Saillans, up onto the Vercors plateau (Resistance sites at La Chapelle, the Luire Cave and Vassieux) basing near Saint-Agnan-en-Vercors, and on into Burgundy near Chalon-sur-Saône for wine and swimming, with a visit to the Hôtel-Dieu at Beaune. The window closes heading north via Guignicourt to the Fontinettes boat-lift near St-Omer, before the return home (covered in the next diary window).
extraction_notes: Travellers limited to Simon, Eve, John and Charles: only these four appear active in this window (the older sons Thomas, William and Peter are referenced remotely — e.g. 'William has just texted that his house move went well' — not present; Tinder the dog is not mentioned). The three 'sketch' pages are NOT route sketches: B_0073_L is a doodle of a field watering machine, B_0075_L is a small arrow showing the loop shape of the Verdon canyon day-trip (no place ordering), and B_0077_R is a glued-in printed canoe-route brochure map of the Drôme (Saillans–Die), not the family's road route — so narrative order was used (no sketch order to override it). Main-route ordering follows the dated narrative: Esparron(Mon/Tue) → Moustiers(Tue–Thu, Verdon excursion Wed) → Saillans/Drôme canoe base(Thu Aug4–, Fri Aug5 canoe) → Saint-Agnan-en-Vercors(Sat–Mon) → NE up Gorge de la Bourne → Saint-Jean-de-Vaux/Chalon Burgundy base(Mon–Thu) → Beaune(Thu Aug11) → Guignicourt(Fri Aug12) → Fontinettes/Arques near St-Omer(Fri Aug12). 'Sailline' read as Saillans (matches the Drôme canoe map showing Saillans). 'St. Jean de [?lux] near Chalon' resolved to Saint-Jean-de-Vaux on the strength of the glued-in 'PISCINE DE ST-JEAN-DE-VAUX' pool ticket and 'PERCEPTION DE GIVRY'. Beaune treated as a main_stop on the northbound leg rather than an excursion because the family said goodbye to the Burgundy base neighbours and left for good that day; the Thursday-night stop before Guignicourt is not stated in the diary. Mellacey/Mercurey and Chalon on B_0080_R are gas-cylinder errands, not route stops. Dates: only Aug 5, 10, 11, 12 are explicit; earlier days back-counted from 'Friday August 5th'. Crossing is inferred dover-calais (no port named in-window).
